news.title: Jaguar Land Rover leases 1.46 lakh sq ft space at Brigade Tech Gardens, Bengaluru
news.para: Jaguar Land Rover India has leased a significant office space in Bengaluru. This expansion at Brigade Tech Gardens marks a major deal driven by global capability centers. The automaker's presence in the city grows substantially. This move highlights the continued demand for specialized talent hubs in automotive research and development.

news.title: Mumbai: MHADA lawyer’s role under lens for delay in Girgaum building redevelopment
news.para: A confidential MHADA report confirms a Section 79A notice to Parekh Estate Pvt Ltd was never revoked, contradicting submissions made to the Bombay High Court. This led to a writ petition being disposed of based on alleged false information. MHADA's legal department recommended terminating the panel advocate's appointment for misleading the court.

news.title: Hyderabad: Developers show interest in Neopolis Kokapet phase-III e-auction
news.para: Over 85 developers have expressed interest in the upcoming e-auction of Neopolis, Kokapet Phase-3, with bidding scheduled for the third week of November. The Hyderabad Metropolitan Development Authority (HMDA) held pre-bid meetings to explain the auction process, regulations, and payment procedures. Plots in Neopolis and Golden Mile layout will be auctioned on multiple dates in November and December.

news.title: Gurugram chokes on construction dust as GRAP-III violations rampant
news.para: Despite GRAP-III restrictions, construction dust continues to pollute Gurugram's air as ongoing projects violate bans. Lax monitoring allows sites to lack dust control measures, with trucks spreading debris unchecked. Residents report persistent dust and nighttime construction, highlighting a significant disconnect between policy and enforcement.

news.title: Blackstone-backed REIT eyes Diamond Plaza mall buyout
news.para: Nexus Select Trust, backed by Blackstone, is in advanced talks to acquire Diamond Plaza mall in Dum Dum for an estimated Rs 450-600 crore. This potential acquisition follows the REIT's recent purchase of South City Mall for Rs 3,250 crore. The deal signifies Nexus Select Trust's continued expansion in India's retail real estate market, particularly in the eastern region.

news.title: CCI Projects to invest ₹700 crore to build residential tower in Mumbai region
news.para: CCI Projects has announced a hefty investment of Rs 700 crore for an opulent housing tower in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region. Rising 53 stories, the Skyleap tower will host 320 luxurious apartments, all nestled within a sprawling 6-acre gated community in Borivali.

news.title: Stonecraft to invest ₹300 crore to build 110 acre township in Telangana
news.para: Stonecraft Group will invest Rs 300 crore in a new 110-acre township at Yadagiri Gutta, Telangana. The project, named Temple Town, includes residential plots and a Senior Living community. The company anticipates a revenue potential of Rs 1,100 crore from this development.

news.title: Smartworks lease out 1.66 lakh sq ft space to Wolters Kluwer in Pune
news.para: Smartworks Coworking Spaces has leased 1.66 lakh sq ft to Wolters Kluwer in Pune, highlighting a significant shift in their business model. The company now sees over 35% of its rental revenue from large enterprise clients, a substantial increase from three years ago. This growth is further evidenced by a 21% year-on-year revenue increase and a positive net-debt position.

news.title: Puravankara lease out over 1.2 lakh sq ft retail space to Ikea at Purva Zentech Park, Bengaluru
news.para: Puravankara has signed a significant agreement with Ikea India to lease over 1.2 lakh sq ft of retail space at its Purva Zentech Park in Bengaluru. This mixed-use development, slated for completion in early 2026, will see IKEA occupy the first two floors.

news.title: Pune: RMC units seek more time to comply with MPCB’s new guidelines
news.para: Ready-mix concrete manufacturers and real estate developers in Pune have requested an extension to comply with new Maharashtra Pollution Control Board (MPCB) guidelines. The regulations mandate canopy coverings for RMC units to control dust, but industry stakeholders cited procedural issues and the need for coordination with the Pune Municipal Corporation for permissions before building these structures.

news.title: Goa: 'Settlement' zone land in ODPs, regional plan to get zoning certificates from TCP
news.para: Goa's TCP department will now issue zoning certificates for land converted under Calangute-Candolim and Arpora-Nagoa-Parra ODPs, provided it's designated as 'Settlement' in Regional Plan 2021. This follows a Supreme Court decision upholding a High Court ruling that set aside the ODPs, emphasizing the need to prevent Goa from becoming a 'concrete jungle'.

news.title: UAE's Arada to acquire 80% of London riverfront development
news.para: UAE-based Arada is making waves in London's real estate scene by acquiring a major interest in the Thameside West project. This colossal 2.5 billion pound initiative is set to provide upwards of 5,000 residences, with ground-breaking expected in 2027.

news.title: Kalyan-Dombivli civic body launches one-window online portal system to prevent illegal constructions
news.para: In response to illegal constructions, Kalyan-Dombivli Municipal Corporation has launched KD SWIFT, Maharashtra's first one-window online system for building permissions. This digital platform streamlines approvals from eight departments within a 28-day deadline, ensuring transparency and preventing malpractice. Citizens can now track applications and receive QR-coded certificates for instant verification, fostering trust and curbing future irregularities.

news.title: Pimpri Chinchwad civic body seals nine RMC plants for violation of environmental norms
news.para: Nine ready-mix concrete plants in Pimpri Chinchwad have been sealed for violating environmental norms, including inadequate dust control and open mixing. This action follows a crackdown by the municipal corporation and the Maharashtra Pollution Control Board due to rising air pollution. Residents had complained about dust and debris from these plants, contributing to a significant drop in air quality.

news.title: Odisha HC allows vigilance court trial in BDA plot allotment scam case
news.para: The Orissa High Court has refused to discharge former Bhubaneswar Development Authority officials accused in a 2014 plot allotment scam. The court found sufficient material for the trial to proceed. The vigilance probe alleges irregular allotment of commercial plots to relatives of officials without advertisement and at low prices.

news.title: Greater Noida authority launches ₹3,600-crore commercial plot scheme
news.para: Greater Noida Industrial Development Authority has launched a major commercial land scheme, offering 37 plots with a combined reserve price of Rs 3,604 crore. These plots, designated for commercial complexes, retail hubs, and mixed-use developments, are available through e-auction. Registrations are open until December 5, with plot sizes and prices varying across several key sectors.

news.title: Allahabad HC junks Noida authority’s extension charge demand against Windsor corporate tower
news.para: Allahabad High Court quashed a Rs 12 crore demand by Noida Authority against Windsor Corporate Tower. The court ruled extension charges must be based on the original lease premium, not current rates. Noida was directed to recalculate charges and refund any excess amount with interest.

news.title: Nagpur civic body seeks freehold status for 332 plots in Shiv Nagar
news.para: The Nagpur Municipal Corporation is proposing to grant freehold status to 332 plots in Shiv Nagar, a move strongly backed by local MLA and the Chief Minister. This decision has sparked accusations of favoritism from plot holders in other city layouts, who are now demanding similar relief ahead of civic elections.

news.title: Mumbai: BMC housing lottery draws 2,037 applicants for 426 homes
news.para: The BMC's lottery for 426 affordable homes saw a modest response with 2,037 applications, significantly lower than MHADA's draws. The flats, priced between Rs 55 lakh and Rs 1 crore, are for EWS and LIG categories. This marks BMC's first independent lottery to streamline the process and avoid processing fees.

news.title: Bar Council of Punjab and Haryana clears M3M India's directors
news.para: The Bar Council of Punjab & Haryana's privilege committee has exonerated M3M India directors Roop Bansal and Yateesh Wahal, along with 17 lawyers, from bench hunting and forum shopping accusations. An inquiry found no evidence of malicious intent or case influence, deeming withdrawals and re-filings routine. M3M India hailed the decision as a validation of procedural integrity.
